meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

family.title: Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services
family.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
family.definition: This major group comprises managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 0. The occupations classified under TEER 0, as those included in this group with zero (0) as the second digit of their code, are considered management occupations.
family.broad_category_title: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
family.major_group_title: Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

units.title: Government managers - health and social policy development and program administration
units.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.major_group_title: Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services
units.sub_major_group_title: Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services
units.definition: Government managers in health and social policy development and program administration plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the development and administration of health care policies, social policies and related programs designed to protect and promote the health and social welfare of individuals and communities. They are employed by all levels of government.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Government managers - health and social policy development and program administration
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Government managers - health and social policy development and program administration
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Government managers in health and social policy development and program administration plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the development and administration of health care policies, social policies and related programs designed to protect and promote the health and social welfare of individuals and communities.
employment_requirements: Government managers in health policy development and program administration
A bachelor's degree or college diploma in health science, hospital administration or public administration or a bachelor's degree in social science is required.
A graduate degree in a health, social science or an administrative discipline may be required.
Several years of experience as a health or social policy researcher, consultant or program officer are usually required.

For some government managers in health policy development and program administration, certification in the relevant health profession is required. For example, city medical officers of health must be physicians.
Government managers in social policy development and program administration
A bachelor's degree or a college diploma in a social science discipline, such as sociology or economics, or an administrative discipline, such as public administration, is required.
A graduate degree in a social science or an administrative discipline may be required.
Several years of experience as a social policy researcher, consultant or program officer are usually required.
additional_information: Progression to senior management positions in the health and social policy fields is possible with experience.

core_competencies.statement: to evaluate complex information and make informed decisions that balance various factors and interests that impact public health
to measure performance of policies or programs, identify areas for improvement and adjust strategies as needed
to identify key issues within health and social policies and address these problems by developing effective strategies and solutions that meet the needs of communities
to clearly communicate policy proposals, engage with stakeholders and present complex information and policy goals
to inspire team members and coordinate efforts to drive the implementation of health and social policies

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Public Affairs and Government Relations
core_competencies.statement: to understand the political and regulatory context of health and social policies, navigate governmental processes and ensure policies are aligned with governmental and public interests

units.title: Government managers - economic analysis, policy development and program administration
units.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.major_group_title: Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services
units.sub_major_group_title: Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services
units.definition: Government managers in economic analysis, policy development and program administration plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate economic policy, research and programs in areas of government activity such as taxation, international trade, labour markets, transportation or agriculture. They also plan and direct policies and programs to promote industrial and commercial business development in urban and rural areas. They are employed by all levels of government.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Government managers - economic analysis, policy development and program administration
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Government managers - economic analysis, policy development and program administration
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Government managers in economic analysis, policy development and program administration plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate economic policy, research and programs in areas of government activity such as taxation, international trade, labour markets, transportation or agriculture. They also plan and direct policies and programs to promote industrial and commercial business development in urban and rural areas.
employment_requirements: A bachelor's degree in economics, business administration, commerce or public administration is required.
A master's degree in economics or a related field may be required.
Several years of experience as an economist, economic development officer or socio-economic researcher are required.
additional_information: Progression to senior management positions is possible with experience.

example_titles.job_title_text: economic policy analysis assistant director - government services
international trade policy assistant director - government services
economic policy analysis chief - government services

core_competencies.statement: to evaluate complex information, consider multiple policy options, anticipate potential impacts and make informed decisions that align with strategic goals
to assess the performance and effectiveness of policies and programs, identify areas for improvement and adjust strategies
to perform accurate financial analyses, assess economic trends, create models and make data-driven decisions
to identify economic issues and develop effective strategies and solutions by logically analyzing problems, possible causes and their potential solutions
to inspire team members and coordinate efforts to drive the implementation of policy development and program administration to achieve program goals

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Public Affairs and Government Relations
core_competencies.statement: to understand the political and regulatory context in which economic policies are implemented and ensure policies align public and governmental objectives

units.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services

units.definition: Government managers in education policy development and program administration plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the development and administration of elementary, secondary and post-secondary education policies and programs. They are employed by all levels of government.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. oasis_profiles.profile_title: Government managers - education policy development and program administration
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Government managers - education policy development and program administration
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Government managers in education policy development and program administration organize, direct, control and evaluate the development and administration of elementary, secondary and post-secondary education policies and programs.
employment_requirements: A bachelor's degree in education is required.
A master's degree in a particular educational specialization may be required.
Several years of combined experience as a school teacher and as a government educational policy researcher, consultant or program officer are usually required.
A provincial teaching certificate is usually required.
additional_information: Progression to senior management positions in education is possible with experience.

example_titles.job_title_text: policy and planning director - education programs
education policy analysis and research director
education programs assistant director - public administration
assistant director of school services - public administration
education curriculum development director - public administration
education policy and research director - public administration
education programs support director - public administration
program development and educational planning director - public administration

core_competencies.statement: to assess the effectiveness of existing policies and identify areas for improvement to ensure policies are practical and align with educational goals
to measure outcomes, review program performance and make necessary adjustments to improve educational quality and address emerging needs
to identify challenges in educational policies or programs, analyze possible causes and apply logical reasoning to create and implement effective interventions
to properly convey policy ideas, clearly present complex information and engage with educators, stakeholders and the public
to inspire and motivate staff, coordinate efforts across various departments and ensure educational initiatives are successfully executed

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Public Affairs and Government Relations
core_competencies.statement: to understand the broader political and regulatory environment in which education policies are implemented and ensure policies align with public expectations and governmental priorities

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Other managers in public administration plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the development of policies and programs which govern the daily operations of legislatures and other activities unique to government such as intergovernmental affairs and elections.
employment_requirements: A bachelor's degree in a social science discipline, law or business administration is required.

Several years of experience in government policy development, research or program administration, or in a professional occupation in social science, law or business administration are usually required.
additional_information: Progression to senior management positions in this field is possible with experience.

example_titles.job_title_text: clerk of the committee - Legislative Assembly
Deputy Clerk of the House of Commons
Deputy Clerk of the Legislative Assembly
Principal Clerk of the House of Commons
Principal Clerk of the Legislative Assembly

core_competencies.statement: to organize efforts, align goals and ensure various stakeholders work together to achieve common objectives
to track progress, assess outcomes and make necessary adjustments to ensure operations run smoothly and objectives are met
to prioritize critical issues and tasks to ensure administrative or legislative processes receive the attention required
to clearly and effectively convey policies, engage with stakeholders and present information to various audiences
to guide, inspire and manage staff towards the implementation of governmental programs and processes to achieve organizational goals

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Public Affairs and Government Relations
core_competencies.statement: to understand the implications of policies and programs, ensuring government activities align with broader public interests and governmental priorities

units.title: Administrators - post-secondary education and vocational training
units.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.major_group_title: Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services
units.sub_major_group_title: Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services
units.definition: Administrators in post-secondary education and vocational training include faculty administrators and registrars of colleges or universities and administrators of vocational training schools. Faculty administrators manage the academic and related activities of faculties of colleges or universities. Registrars manage registration activities and academic records systems of colleges or universities. Administrators of vocational training schools manage the operations of vocational schools specializing in trades, technology, business or other vocational subjects.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Administrators - post-secondary education and vocational training
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Faculty administrators manage the academic and related activities of faculties of colleges and universities.
main_duties: Plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate academic and related activities of a college or university faculty
Recommend and approve faculty appointments
Recommend curriculum revisions and additions and approve scheduling of courses
Direct, through subordinate staff, activities such as research and curriculum development
Plan, administer and control budgets for projects, programs, support services and equipment
Advise president or rector of university or college
Participate in activities of various faculty and college committees.
employment_requirements: Faculty administrators require a graduate degree in a field related to the academic faculty andseveral years of experience as a university professor or college teacher.
additional_information: There is no mobility among the various types of administrators in this group.
Progression to some senior management positions in education is possible with experience.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Administrators - post-secondary education and vocational training
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Registrars manage registration activities and academic record systems of colleges and universities.
main_duties: Plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the registration activities and academic records system of a college or university
Review registration statistics and consult with faculty officials to develop registration policies
Direct activities of staff and others engaged in compiling information on courses, schedules and requirements for admission and graduation.
employment_requirements: Registrars require an undergraduate degree in business administration or a related field andseveral years of experience in registration administration.
additional_information: There is no mobility among the various types of administrators in this group.
Progression to some senior management positions in education is possible with experience.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Administrators - post-secondary education and vocational training
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Administrators of vocational training schools manage the operations of private vocational schools specializing in trades, technology, business and other vocational subjects.
main_duties: Plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the activities of a private college or institute specializing in trades, technology or business
Develop curriculum for training programs
Consult with government regulatory and licensing agencies to ensure conformance to provincial standards
Plan, administer and control budgets for projects, programs, support services and equipment
May recruit and hire teachers and other staff
May teach.
employment_requirements: Administrators of vocational training schools usually require an undergraduate degree in business administration orexpertise and certification in a subject of instruction.
additional_information: There is no mobility among the various types of administrators in this group.
Progression to some senior management positions in education is possible with experience.

units.title: School principals and administrators of elementary and secondary education
units.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.major_group_title: Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services
units.sub_major_group_title: Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services
units.definition: School principals plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the activities of teachers and other staff of an elementary or secondary school. They are employed by public and private schools. Administrators of elementary and secondary education plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the academic affairs of a school system. They are employed by school boards.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: School principals and administrators of elementary and secondary education
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: School principals plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the activities of teachers and other staff of an elementary or secondary school.
main_duties: Plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate, through department heads or supervisors, the activities of teachers and support staff
Review programs to ensure conformance to school board or provincial standards and develop programs within limits of authority
Co-ordinate teaching activities of the institution by making personnel assignments, determining class size and preparing timetables
Organize and maintain procedures for the keeping of records
Prepare and administer institution or program budget
Direct and co-ordinate school maintenance services and the use of school facilities
May recruit and hire teachers and other staff
May teach.
employment_requirements: A bachelor's degree in education is required.
A master's degree in education may be required.
Several years of experience as a senior teacher or department head are required.
A teacher's certificate for the province of employment is required.
School principals may require a principal's certificate.
additional_information: There is little mobility between institutional settings, for example, between elementary and secondary schools.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Administrators of elementary and secondary education
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: School principals and administrators of elementary and secondary education
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Administrators of elementary and secondary education plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the academic affairs of a school system.
main_duties: Plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate, through school principals, the academic affairs of a school district
Evaluate curriculum, teaching methods and community participation in programs for a school district and revise programs as required
Develop and administer programs for the education and training of students and adults
Make recommendations concerning the annual operating budget of a school district
Direct or supervise the recruitment, appointment, training, evaluation and promotion of teaching personnel.
employment_requirements: A bachelor's degree in education is required.
A master's degree in education may be required.
Several years of experience as a senior teacher or department head are required.
Administrators of elementary and secondary education may require a supervisory officer certificate.
additional_information: There is little mobility between institutional settings, for example, between elementary and secondary schools.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Managers in social, community and correctional services
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Managers in social, community and correctional services
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Managers in social, community and correctional services plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate the programs and activities of social service and community agencies, correctional institutions, counselling departments, labour organizations, professional associations, political parties and non-governmental organizations.
employment_requirements: Managers in social, community and correctional services usually require a master's degree in a social science or administrative discipline andseveral years of experience in a related occupation, such as a community and social service worker, social or health policy researcher, consultant or program officer, probation or parole officer, or social worker.
Managers of associations and membership organizations require extensive experience in a related occupation, trade or industry.
additional_information: Progression to senior management positions in social, community and correctional services is possible with additional training and experience.

example_titles.job_title_text: assistant vice-president – health, education, social and community services and membership organizations

core_competencies.statement: to ensure the team operates effectively, including recruiting, training and supervising staff in order to meet organizational objectives
to appropriately respond to the social dynamics and the needs of diverse groups within the community
to recognize problems early to develop and implement appropriate solutions, request funding or research potential solutions
to clearly and accurately articulate program goals, engage staff and stakeholders
to inspire confidence, manage conflicts and foster a collaborative environment to achieve organizational goals and support the community effectively
to understand concepts and practices to properly manage social, community or correctional services, including strategic planning, budgeting and optimizing resource use

units.title: Commissioned police officers and related occupations in public protection services
units.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.major_group_title: Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services
units.sub_major_group_title: Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services
units.definition: Commissioned police officers plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate police force administration and police activities such as maintaining law and order and detecting and preventing crime. They are employed by municipal, provincial and federal governments. This unit group includes officers from the rank of staff sergeant to police commissioner. Commissioned officers in the railway police, deputy chief coroners, and deputy chief medical examiners are also included in this unit group.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. oasis_profiles.profile_title: Commissioned police officers and related occupations in public protection services
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Commissioned police officers and related occupations in public protection services
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Commissioned police officers and related occupations in public protection services plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate police force administration and police activities such as maintaining law and order and detecting and preventing crime. This unit group includes officers from the rank of staff sergeant to police commissioner. Commissioned officers in the railway police, deputy chief coroners, and deputy chief medical examiners are also included in this unit group.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A university degree in the social sciences or in business administration is usually required.
Several years of experience as a police officer are required.
additional_information: Depending on provincial and territorial legislation, deputy chief coroners and deputy chief medical examiners may act as or be appointed to the position of chief coroner and chief medical examiner in their absence.

core_competencies.statement: to align various activities, groups or individuals in order to ensure resources are used efficiently and efforts are integrated to address public safety concerns
to assess progress, identify areas for improvement and make necessary adjustments to enhance public protection services
to oversee various aspects of police force administration while simultaneously responding to emerging situations, managing teams and coordinating complex operations
to quickly and accurately identify problems, such as emerging crime trends, operational inefficiencies or public safety issues
to inspires and motivates officers, promoting a culture of accountability, integrity and commitment to community safety

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Public Safety and Security
core_competencies.statement: to understand advanced principles and methods of public safety to anticipate and respond to potential threats, ensure the safety of communities and adhere to best practices in crime prevention and response

units.title: Fire chiefs and senior firefighting officers
units.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.major_group_title: Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services
units.sub_major_group_title: Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services
units.definition: Fire chiefs and senior firefighting officers plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate firefighting operations and fire prevention activities in fire departments. They are employed by municipal and federal governments and by industrial establishments with firefighting services.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Fire chiefs and senior firefighting officers
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Fire chiefs and senior firefighting officers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Fire chiefs and senior firefighting officers plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate firefighting operations and fire prevention activities in fire departments.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of a college program in fire protection technology, fire science or a related field is usually required.
Completion of a provincial or municipal firefighters training course is required.
A minimum of ten years of experience as a firefighter is usually required before becoming eligible for a senior officer position.

core_competencies.statement: to ensure each team understands their specific role, works in sync with other units and follows a structured operational plan
to ensure firefighters are well-prepared, perform their duties effectively and are supported in their professional development and well-being
to efficiently handle coordinating emergency responses, managing resources and overseeing staff under high-pressure conditions

core_competencies.competency: Pattern Organization Speed and Perceptual Speed
core_competencies.statement: to quickly analyze and respond to complex and rapidly evolving emergency situations to direct firefighters and citizens to safety
to guide firefighting teams, make strategic decisions during emergencies and ensure effective execution of fire prevention and response plans

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Public Safety and Security
core_competencies.statement: to understand principles and methods of public safety to establish and review firefighting operations policies regarding potential risks and alignment with broader public safety objectives

units.title: Commissioned officers of the Canadian Armed Forces
units.category: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in education, law and social, community and government services
units.major_group_title: Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services
units.sub_major_group_title: Managers in public administration, in education and social and community services and in public protection services
units.definition: Commissioned officers of the Canadian Armed Forces plan, organize, command, direct, control and evaluate, the operations of their organization in relation to established objectives. Senior commissioned officers formulate military and strategic policies which establish the direction to be taken by the Canadian Forces. All ranks of commissioned officers in the Royal Canadian Air Force, Canadian Army, and Royal Canadian Navy are included in this unit group.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Commissioned officers of the Canadian Armed Forces
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Commissioned officers of the Canadian Armed Forces
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Commissioned officers of the Canadian Armed Forces plan, organize, command, direct, control and evaluate, the operations of their organization in relation to established objectives. Senior commissioned officers formulate military and strategic policies which establish the direction to be taken by the Canadian Forces. All ranks of commissioned officers in the Royal Canadian Air Force, Canadian Army, and Royal Canadian Navy are included in this unit group.
employment_requirements: A university degree is usually required.
Completion of military training is required.
Experience as a non-commissioned member of the armed forces and demonstrated leadership ability may substitute for a university degree.
Senior ranks, such as colonel, require several years of experience.
additional_information: Senior commissioned officers are classified according to their military rank.

core_competencies.statement: to ensure personnel are well-prepared, motivated and deployed effectively to meet operational goals
to assess performance and progress of military staff, make necessary adjustments and ensure operations are complying to Armed Forces standards
to effectively handle coordinating operations, making strategic decisions and overseeing various units during critical missions or in high-pressure situations
to identify problems, whether tactical, operational or strategic, to address issues promptly and accurately
to command military personnel, maintain discipline within units and make strategic decisions to achieve mission success
to understand how to manage and distribute resources, supplies and equipment to support the operational readiness of military units and missions
